text.title: Audit-Grade Code Review Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Audit-Grade Code Review Checklist methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale. Hard rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: One PR per checklist instance; batching multiple PRs into one evidence record breaks audit traceability.
rule.rationale: Audit-grade evidence is per-change; bundling defeats the purpose of the trail.

rule.statement: Each PR has ≥2 named reviewers; single-reviewer reviews are flagged as audit gaps.
rule.rationale: Single-reviewer setups encode the reviewer's biases without correction.

rule.statement: Reviewer MUST verify request/response shape against the published API spec; undocumented breaking changes are blockers.
rule.rationale: Without contract-conformance checks, breaking changes leak into prod and surface during integration.

rule.statement: Reviewer MUST grep the diff for hardcoded secrets and confirm auth scope on touched endpoints.
rule.rationale: Secret leakage is the most common audit finding; auth scope drift is the most common security regression.

rule.statement: Reviewer outcome + timestamp + check results MUST be appended to a per-PR evidence log; mental-only review is rejected.
rule.rationale: Evidence-only-in-Slack does not survive audit; the structured log is the artefact auditors read.
